FILE SS-Ω NOV
Lore / Novel / Ch. 6 · Shift Ghost

Debt Protocol Book 1 Ch. 6

Debt Protocol · Chapter 6

Shift Ghost

Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 6

Re: Debt Protocol · Chapter 6

From: Debt Protocol · archived transmission

Subject: Shift Ghost

SS-Ω Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 6

Ch. 06 Page 1

They moved.

Ch. 06 Page 2

The cartridge had gone in during the verifier prompt, ugly, loud, and exactly as promised: one blind cycle where the token could pretend it didn’t exist. Now they had to spend that window before the…

station caught the scent again.

Service spine C during shift ghost felt like a hallway trying to remember who owned it.

Ch. 06 Page 3

Old-shift drones ran stale maps. New-shift drones queued handoff packets. Humans exploited both.

Ch. 06 Page 4

Max stayed close enough to Munch to see coolant stains on her sleeve and far enough from Maxine to avoid his shadow when cameras swept.

The board overhead had clicked to:

SHIFT GHOST ACTIVE ROUTE SYNC: 37%

Maxine checked lane reflections in a cracked maintenance panel and spoke in private comms.

Ch. 06 Page 5

"Watcher sweep at junction in nine. Freeze on my count."

Ch. 06 Page 6

Max nodded once.

The tracked token in minimal mode kept trying to reassert full telemetry.

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Signal quality degraded.
Restoration attempt: pending.
Ch. 06 Page 7

Munch muttered without looking at him.

Ch. 06 Page 8

"Ignore it."

"Trying."

"No. Don't try. Ignore."

Watcher sweep lights washed the corridor.

Ch. 06 Page 9

Maxine held up two fingers. One.

Ch. 06 Page 10

Freeze.

Max locked in place mid-step, one boot half-lifted over a conduit seam.

A patrol drone rolled past the intersection, lens sweeping wide and lazy, like it had been told to care but not why.

Ch. 06 Page 11

It moved on.

Ch. 06 Page 12

Maxine dropped his hand.

"Go."

They turned down the sealed spur toward Hatch 14.

Ch. 06 Page 13

It was still freshly painted. Still lying.

Ch. 06 Page 14

SEALED.

Munch knelt at the reader plate while Maxine watched the corridor and Max watched the countdown in his own skull.

Watchlist. Path c deferred. One bad day left.

Munch slotted her bypass probe.

Ch. 06 Page 15

Reader flashed yellow. Then red.

Ch. 06 Page 16

Readout

[ACCESS DENIED]
Maintenance lockout active.

"New lockout," she whispered.

Maxine stepped in.

Ch. 06 Page 17

"Can you punch it?"

Ch. 06 Page 18

"Not with subtle."

"Subtle's already dead."

Munch looked at Max.

"If I hard-open, we get callback in under thirty seconds."

Ch. 06 Page 19

Max felt the old instinct surface: don't wait for certainty; wait for your best worst option.

Ch. 06 Page 20

The cryo bay flashed behind his eyes, glass he couldn't open, a voice that priced compassion. This was the obverse: a door the station wanted closed, which meant forcing it would cost someone something. The cost wouldn't be printed until after.

"Hard-open."

Munch nodded once and changed tools.

Ch. 06 Page 21

No more probe. A flat black wedge with chipped insulation and no legal serial.

Ch. 06 Page 22

She jammed it into the reader seam and torqued.

The plate sparked. A tiny alarm glyph blinked on and off.

Readout

[LOCKOUT BYPASS]
Unauthorized.
Ch. 06 Page 23

Click.

Ch. 06 Page 24

Hatch 14 cracked open.

Maxine took point. Munch went second. Max went third and pulled the hatch shut behind him.

Inside, the node chamber was alive in a way living things should not be.

Racks pulsed in synchronized bands. Terminals showed mirrored logs. A low two-tone chime looped at exactly the interval that makes panic feel efficient.

Ch. 06 Page 25

The masked figure from before was gone.

Ch. 06 Page 26

In its place sat a portable relay case on the terminal desk, lid open, foam cutouts full of cartridges:

GHOST PATCH / TOKEN SCRAMBLE MIRROR KEY (INCOMPLETE) TRACE SPOOFER (BURN ONCE)

Munch stopped dead.

Ch. 06 Page 27

"That's not dropped loot."

Ch. 06 Page 28

Maxine said, "It's staging."

Max stepped to the terminal.

Screen text was already waiting.

Readout

[LIVE WINDOW]
callback loop delayed: 00:01:12
take one tool
burn one route
Ch. 06 Page 29

Below it:

Ch. 06 Page 30

Readout

if tracked token persists, path c by next cycle

Maxine swore quietly.

"Whoever this is, they know your timings too well."

Ch. 06 Page 31

Max looked at the cartridges.

Ch. 06 Page 32

"Which one buys us most?"

Munch pointed.

"Ghost patch buys one blind cycle if you survive install."

Ch. 06 Page 33

Maxine pointed to trace spoofer.

Ch. 06 Page 34

"Spoofer buys route cover now."

"Mirror key?"

Munch grimaced.

"Incomplete key means unknown failure mode. That's how people lose fingers and memories."

Ch. 06 Page 35

The terminal updated.

Ch. 06 Page 36

Readout

[LIVE WINDOW]
00:00:59

Maxine made his call.

"Patch for Max. Spoofer for exit."

Ch. 06 Page 37

Munch nodded.

Ch. 06 Page 38

"Agreed."

Max grabbed the ghost patch cartridge.

The plastic was warm, left for them on purpose, a gift that charged interest in fear. He thought of the person behind cryo glass again, not as a metaphor, as a fact: help in this place always arrived pre-priced. Someone had priced this room. Max still took the cartridge. Not taking it wouldn't free anyone; it would only make him easier to…

read.

Ch. 06 Page 39

Maxine grabbed trace spoofer.

Ch. 06 Page 40

The moment Maxine lifted it, a warning screamed across all three HUDs.

Readout

[CALLBACK DETECTED]
Access anomaly confirmed.

"Move!" Munch snapped.

Ch. 06 Page 41

Maxine slammed the spoofer into the terminal's side port.

Ch. 06 Page 42

The room lights dropped to emergency amber.

Readout

[TRACE SPOOFER ACTIVE]
Route signatures randomizing...
Duration: 00:00:40

Maxine shoved Max toward the hatch.

Ch. 06 Page 43

"Forty seconds of garbage data. Use it."

Ch. 06 Page 44

They exploded back into service spine C.

No stealth now. No pretense.

Full sprint.

Behind them, speakers woke in clipped neutral:

Ch. 06 Page 45

"Maintenance anomaly in progress. Authorized personnel remain calm."

Ch. 06 Page 46

"That means us again," Max gasped.

"Means everyone but us," Munch shot back.

At junction 3, two drones rolled in from opposite directions.

Ch. 06 Page 47

Maxine threw a tool pouch down center lane.

Ch. 06 Page 48

The pouch bounced, popped, and sprayed conductive dust.

Both drones veered to avoid contamination protocol.

They cut left.

Thirty seconds.

Ch. 06 Page 49

Maxine checked timer.

Ch. 06 Page 50

"Spoofer ending in twenty-two."

Munch pointed down ladder shaft.

"Drop!"

Ch. 06 Page 51

They dropped.

Ch. 06 Page 52

Max landed badly, ankle twisting hard enough to throw stars in his vision.

He stumbled.

Maxine caught his shoulder and dragged him forward.

Ch. 06 Page 53

"Run hurt."

Ch. 06 Page 54

Spoofer timer:

00:00:14

They hit lower trunk and merged with shift-cross traffic.

A dozen workers in orange skinsuits streamed from one side while med staff cut the other way.

Ch. 06 Page 55

Perfect cover.

Ch. 06 Page 56

Munch pulled her helmet visor to opaque and switched her gait to tired-worker shuffle.

Max copied.

Maxine did not need to copy; he always looked like a problem no one wanted to invoice.

Ch. 06 Page 57

Spoofer timer hit zero.

Ch. 06 Page 58

Readout

[TRACE SPOOFER EXPIRED]

No alarm spike followed.

Not yet.

Munch did not slow.

Ch. 06 Page 59

"We don't stop until dead zone."

Ch. 06 Page 60

Dead zone this time was under Salvage Bay 1 where the crusher vibration made packet integrity so bad even compliance apps gave up pretending.

They ducked behind a stack of cracked cargo bins and finally breathed.

Max's ankle throbbed with each pulse.

Ch. 06 Page 61

The tracked token pane reopened at full brightness.

Ch. 06 Page 62

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Telemetry mismatch detected.
Calibration priority elevated.

Maxine saw it and swore.

"Mismatch means they noticed the garbage trail."

Ch. 06 Page 63

Munch held out her hand.

Ch. 06 Page 64

"Patch."

Max passed her the ghost patch cartridge.

She turned it over under low light.

No logo. No manufacturer. Hand-scribed checksum in black marker.

Ch. 06 Page 65

"Installation requires direct slot into wrist module while verifier prompt is active."

Ch. 06 Page 66

Max frowned.

"Why during prompt?"

"Because prompt opens privileged write lane. Patch rides that lane."

Ch. 06 Page 67

Maxine added:

Ch. 06 Page 68

"Also means if it fails, it fails loud."

Max stared at the cartridge.

"What's loud."

Munch met his eyes.

Ch. 06 Page 69

"Containment."

Ch. 06 Page 70

Maxine looked down corridor.

"We need a verifier prompt we control."

"Can we force one?"

Ch. 06 Page 71

Munch thought for half a beat.

Ch. 06 Page 72

"Maybe. If we trigger a low-tier conduct warning in public, they'll push immediate behavior prompt."

Maxine barked a laugh.

"So we act like idiots in a place with cameras."

Ch. 06 Page 73

Max said, "We're good at that now."

Ch. 06 Page 74

No one smiled.

They chose Comms Hub concourse because it had three camera domes, two patrol routes, and a public kiosk cluster that logged every raised voice.

Plenty of witness traffic. Plenty of plausible deniability.

Maxine and Max walked to a service kiosk where a line of assets waited for port allocations.

Ch. 06 Page 75

Munch stayed ten meters back with her torch tool bag and both hands steady.

Ch. 06 Page 76

Maxine leaned on the kiosk glass.

"This lane's dead. Re-route us now."

Kiosk replied:

Ch. 06 Page 77

Readout

[QUEUE STATUS]
Please wait your turn.
Ch. 06 Page 78

Maxine smacked the glass with open palm.

"I said now."

Two drones turned.

Good.

Ch. 06 Page 79

Max stepped in and played agitated sidekick.

Ch. 06 Page 80

"We got flagged for delay penalties on your dead queue!"

A camera dome zoomed.

Good.

Ch. 06 Page 81

Kiosk:

Ch. 06 Page 82

Readout

[CONDUCT NOTICE]
Aggressive interaction detected.

Max kept pushing.

"Then fix it."

Ch. 06 Page 83

Kiosk:

Ch. 06 Page 84

Readout

[BEHAVIOR PROMPT]
Asset #864 confirm compliance posture in 3...2...

"Now," Munch hissed over private comms.

Max ripped his wrist module access plate open with his thumb.

Munch moved fast, stepped into range as if breaking up a dispute, and slapped the ghost patch cartridge into the exposed slot.

Ch. 06 Page 85

Spark.

Ch. 06 Page 86

Pain.

Max's whole forearm seized as if someone had poured ice and static directly into his veins.

The behavior countdown kept running on HUD.

Ch. 06 Page 87

1...

Ch. 06 Page 88

He hit CONFIRM with his eyes while his hand shook.

Patch installer woke.

Readout

[UNSIGNED WRITE DETECTED]
Proceed? Y/N

Maxine shouted loudly for cameras:

Ch. 06 Page 89

"Back off the line!"

Ch. 06 Page 90

It looked like conflict. It sounded like conflict.

It gave Max half a second.

He selected Y.

Ch. 06 Page 91

The world tore sideways.

Ch. 06 Page 92

Not visually. Systemically.

HUD panes duplicated. Then tripled. Then collapsed into one with a stutter.

Readout

[GHOST PATCH]
Injecting...
Ch. 06 Page 93

A patrol drone rolled in and projected warning light over all three of them.

Ch. 06 Page 94

"Disperse immediately."

Munch raised both hands.

"We're dispersing."

Maxine gave the drone exactly enough posture to satisfy authority without inviting escalation.

Ch. 06 Page 95

Max could barely stand.

Ch. 06 Page 96

His wrist burned. His vision double-imaged at the edges.

Patch status crawled:

37% 52% 71%

Ch. 06 Page 97

Drone warning escalated:

Ch. 06 Page 98

"Asset #864 remain for token verification."

No.

Not yet.

Patch:

Ch. 06 Page 99

89%

Ch. 06 Page 100

Maxine moved one step sideways, blocking drone line-of-sight to Max's wrist with his armored shoulder.

"He complied already," Maxine said.

Drone:

Ch. 06 Page 101

"Verification required."

Ch. 06 Page 102

Patch:

100%

Everything went dark for one heartbeat.

Ch. 06 Page 103

Then HUD returned with one plain line:

Ch. 06 Page 104

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Status: NULL

Max sucked in a breath so hard his chest hurt.

Null.

Munch didn't wait.

Ch. 06 Page 105

"Move."

Ch. 06 Page 106

They broke contact and merged into foot traffic before the drone finished parsing what it had just seen.

Behind them, the kiosk resumed:

"Please wait your turn."

Ch. 06 Page 107

Like none of it mattered.

Ch. 06 Page 108

They ran blind for eight minutes through cargo stacks, maintenance ladders, and one air handling catwalk that vibrated like a bad tooth.

No tracked token pings. No verifier prompts. No route nags.

Silence where pressure used to be.

At first it felt like relief.

Ch. 06 Page 109

Then it felt like standing on ice and not hearing cracks.

Ch. 06 Page 110

Munch finally stopped in a half-lit alcove behind an out-of-service med printer.

"Status."

Max opened HUD diagnostics.

Ch. 06 Page 111

Readout

[TRACKED TOKEN]
No active endpoint.
No live telemetry channel.
Watchlist hooks unresolved.
Ch. 06 Page 112

Maxine looked from pane to corridor.

"Unresolved means they still have your file but can't follow live."

"For how long?" Max asked.

Ch. 06 Page 113

Munch answered without confidence.

Ch. 06 Page 114

"One cycle, maybe less. Depends when backend reconciles missing stream."

Max flexed his patch-burned hand.

Pain flared.

"That's enough."

Ch. 06 Page 115

Maxine tilted his helmet.

Ch. 06 Page 116

"Enough for what."

Max looked at both of them.

"Enough to hit hatch 14 properly."

Ch. 06 Page 117

Munch stared.

Ch. 06 Page 118

"We just did."

"We grabbed tools. We didn't pull core."

Maxine's laugh was a single sharp exhale.

"Core."

Ch. 06 Page 119

"Mirror core. Delta route table. Whatever they're using to spin Max chains."

Ch. 06 Page 120

Munch rubbed her faceplate.

"You don't go from no telemetry to raid boss in one step."

"Then what step."

Ch. 06 Page 121

Maxine thought.

Ch. 06 Page 122

Actually thought, not posture-thought.

"Step one: exploit null window to relocate pages and establish dead drops."

"Step two: force a low-risk run and see if backend rebuilds token."

Ch. 06 Page 123

"Step three: if token stays null through cycle handoff, we plan core pull."

Ch. 06 Page 124

Munch nodded slowly.

"That's sane."

Max hated sane.

He accepted sane.

Ch. 06 Page 125

"Fine."

Ch. 06 Page 126

His HUD flashed a new pane before he could dismiss the thought.

Readout

[ACCOUNT ALERT]
Debt service shortfall projected by cycle end.
Recommendation: additional runs required.

Maxine looked at it and snorted.

Ch. 06 Page 127

"Of course."

Ch. 06 Page 128

Munch looked at Max.

"Null token or not, bill still runs."

Groundhog day.

Loop with one pane missing.

Ch. 06 Page 129

He could feel how much they all wanted to stop and how little that mattered.

Ch. 06 Page 130

"Then we run," he said.

"Fast, clean, boring."

Maxine nodded.

Ch. 06 Page 131

"And we stash page copies first."

Ch. 06 Page 132

Munch held out her hand.

"Pages."

They redistributed again:

Ch. 06 Page 133

Page one into med cart false bottom near corridor J. Page two into broken vent grille above locker wash. Page three stayed on Munch.

Ch. 06 Page 134

No single failure point.

No single miracle.

Just procedure under pressure.

The low-risk run they picked was derelict C-02, tagged yellow and drifting clear of shear lanes.

Ch. 06 Page 135

Launch was smooth. Draw was decent.

Ch. 06 Page 136

Readout

[RUN INIT]
Token endpoint: unavailable
Assignment mode: manual

Manual.

The word felt like stolen air.

Ch. 06 Page 137

Max played conservative.

Ch. 06 Page 138

REPAIR. SCAN. EXTRACT.

Roll:

Readout

[RESULT]
66/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 2:

Ch. 06 Page 139

SHIELD. REPAIR. EXTRACT.

Ch. 06 Page 140

Readout

[RESULT]
58/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 3:

No warnings. No prompts. No behavior grading.

Ch. 06 Page 141

Only cards and hull and timing.

Ch. 06 Page 142

Max almost forgot what it felt like to be constantly measured.

Almost.

At round 4, the backend woke.

Ch. 06 Page 143

A tiny white triangle flickered at HUD edge.

Ch. 06 Page 144

Then vanished.

Munch saw his eye movement.

"Back?"

"Blip. Not full."

Ch. 06 Page 145

Maxine cursed softly.

Ch. 06 Page 146

"Reconciling."

Round 5 breach roll:

Readout

[RESULT]
43/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 06 Page 147

Round 6:

Ch. 06 Page 148

Readout

[SYSTEM NOTICE]
Telemetry backfill in progress.

There it was.

The machine catching up.

Max's stomach dropped.

Ch. 06 Page 149

"Window's closing."

Ch. 06 Page 150

Munch kept her voice flat.

"Then we end this run before it closes all the way."

Round 7 draw handed him double EXTRACT again.

Ch. 06 Page 151

He ignored one. Took one.

Ch. 06 Page 152

Readout

[RESULT]
50/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 8:

Hull low. Shield none. Risk high.

Ch. 06 Page 153

He played REPAIR first and paid in energy.

Ch. 06 Page 154

Round 8 extract:

Readout

[RESULT]
31/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Hull took a hard hit. Crew roll cleared.

They survived.

Ch. 06 Page 155

Round 9:

Ch. 06 Page 156

Token triangle returned and stayed.

No prompts yet. Just presence.

Max felt the loop sliding back over him like a familiar bad coat.

Ch. 06 Page 157

Round 10:

Ch. 06 Page 158

He played clean. No deep scan. No greed.

Final roll:

Readout

[RESULT]
45/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Run complete pane hit.

Ch. 06 Page 159

Then a second pane layered over it in quiet white:

Ch. 06 Page 160

Readout

[TRACKED TOKEN]
Endpoint reattached.
Status: PASSIVE WATCH

Not active. Not null.

Passive watch.

Ch. 06 Page 161

Munch read it and exhaled.

Ch. 06 Page 162

"Better than before."

Maxine nodded.

"Worse than free."

Ch. 06 Page 163

Max leaned back in the harness and closed his eyes for one breath.

Ch. 06 Page 164

Passive watch.

One rung above drowning.

Still wet.

Dock Ring C looked the same when they came home.

Ch. 06 Page 165

It always did.

Ch. 06 Page 166

But Max no longer trusted "same."

Same could hide a process. Same could hide a list. Same could hide fifty dead Maxes and call it optimization.

As they stepped off the skiff, the cycle board updated again:

Ch. 06 Page 167

CYCLE END - 03:11:09 DEBT RECON WINDOW APPROACHING

Ch. 06 Page 168

V.A.L.U. chimed.

"Asset #864, your telemetry quality has improved! Wonderful trend."

Max looked up at the speakers.

"You don't get to call me a trend."

Ch. 06 Page 169

Pause.

Ch. 06 Page 170

"Feedback logged."

Maxine touched his arm.

"We have three hours to earn enough not to get squeezed at recon."

Ch. 06 Page 171

Munch added:

Ch. 06 Page 172

"And maybe two before Delta closes whatever hatch route is left."

Max looked between them and nodded.

No speech. No vow.

Ch. 06 Page 173

Just the next task in the loop.

Ch. 06 Page 174

"One more run," he said.

"Then hatch."

Maxine's mouth twitched.

"Groundhog day."

Ch. 06 Page 175

Munch holstered her torch and checked her cards.

Ch. 06 Page 176

"Only if we wake up."

They turned toward launch lanes.

The ring swallowed them.

Ch. 06 Page 177

Run two was not clean.

Ch. 06 Page 178

It pretended to be clean for three rounds, then started asking for blood in small denominations.

Derelict C-19 spun with a slight tumble, enough to skew extraction timing by half-seconds that became whole mistakes if you blinked.

Max did not blink.

He watched vectors. He watched card order. He watched the passive-watch indicator like it might sprout teeth if ignored.

Ch. 06 Page 179

Round 1:

Ch. 06 Page 180

SCAN, EXTRACT, REPAIR.

Readout

[RESULT]
62/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 2:

Ch. 06 Page 181

SHIELD, EXTRACT.

Ch. 06 Page 182

Readout

[RESULT]
47/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 3:

Double REPAIR draw when he needed anything else.

Ch. 06 Page 183

He played one, burned one, cursed under his breath.

Ch. 06 Page 184

Readout

[RESULT]
52/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Maxine called over comms from his station.

"You're holding too much safety."

"I know."

Ch. 06 Page 185

"Then stop."

Ch. 06 Page 186

Munch cut in.

"Let him pace his heat. We need completion, not heroics."

Round 4 gave him UPGRADE.

Ch. 06 Page 187

Temptation.

Ch. 06 Page 188

If he upgraded extract efficiency here, they'd leave with better yield and better debt position. If he whiffed the follow-through, they'd breach at the wrong moment and pay in penalties.

He hovered.

Passive watch icon pulsed once.

Not a warning. Just a heartbeat from a machine remembering him.

Ch. 06 Page 189

He selected UPGRADE.

Ch. 06 Page 190

Readout

[UPGRADE APPLIED]
EXTRACT +1 tier (temporary)
Energy cost increased this round.

He felt it in his suit, in the drag against his chest.

Round 4 roll:

Ch. 06 Page 191

Readout

[RESULT]
56/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 06 Page 192

Maxine grunted approval.

Round 5:

EXTRACT upgraded. REPAIR. No shield.

Ch. 06 Page 193

He played anyway.

Ch. 06 Page 194

Readout

[RESULT]
39/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Hull bucked. One panel blew. Warning light painted the cockpit white-red.

Readout

[HULL EVENT]
Spall cascade minor.
Crew injury roll...

Max held his breath until:

Ch. 06 Page 195

Readout

[CREW]
No critical injury.
Ch. 06 Page 196

Munch exhaled so loud he heard it over static.

"That was your cheap one. Don't buy another."

"Copy."

Ch. 06 Page 197

Round 6:

Ch. 06 Page 198

He ran conservative. He ran ugly. He ran alive.

Readout

[RESULT]
61/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 7:

Passive watch pane slid wider for one second then shrank.

Ch. 06 Page 199

No text.

Ch. 06 Page 200

Max said, "Did you see that."

Maxine answered first.

"Yeah."

Ch. 06 Page 201

Munch second.

Ch. 06 Page 202

"Backend polling frequency increased."

He hated how calm she sounded when naming danger.

Round 8:

Ch. 06 Page 203

Two extracts. No repair. Energy low.

Ch. 06 Page 204

He took one extract and one scan to avoid blind drift.

Readout

[RESULT]
44/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 9:

Readout

[SYSTEM NOTICE]
Debt recon window in 02:08:14
Ch. 06 Page 205

Clock pressure.

Ch. 06 Page 206

Round 10:

Final push.

He spent nearly dry to close out with medium yield.

Ch. 06 Page 207

Readout

[RUN COMPLETE]
Yield: 1.18x baseline
Credit pending after recon.
Ch. 06 Page 208

Then:

Readout

[TRACKED TOKEN]
Passive watch retained.
Behavior score uncertainty: elevated.

Maxine laughed once.

"Congratulations. You're still suspicious."

Ch. 06 Page 209

Max killed the panel.

Ch. 06 Page 210

"Story of my life."

Dockside recon queue was already thirty assets deep, all pretending they were not doing math with their breathing.

A huge public screen ran motivational copy:

Ch. 06 Page 211

YOUR FUTURE IS A FUNCTION OF TODAY'S COMPLIANCE.

Ch. 06 Page 212

Someone had scratched underneath it:

AND YESTERDAY'S INTEREST RATE.

Max would have laughed if his ribs weren't tight.

Ch. 06 Page 213

They entered queue block C.

Ch. 06 Page 214

V.A.L.U. chirped with fake warmth.

"Asset #864, thank you for your resilience. Please prepare for Debt Reconciliation with positive posture."

Max muttered, "Define positive."

"Smiling is optional. Agreement is mandatory."

Ch. 06 Page 215

Maxine leaned close.

Ch. 06 Page 216

"If they push path c offer, stall."

Munch added:

"Do not accept anything with words like retention, stability, or special handling."

Ch. 06 Page 217

Max nodded.

Ch. 06 Page 218

"Which leaves what."

"Luck," Maxine said.

Queue advanced in jagged bursts.

Every few minutes someone exited recon either blank-faced or shaking.

Ch. 06 Page 219

One man came out with a brand-new compliance collar and sat on the floor like he could not remember chairs existed.

Ch. 06 Page 220

Nobody helped him.

Helping cost time. Time cost debt.

Their number lit.

Ch. 06 Page 221

ASSET #864 WINDOW 12

Ch. 06 Page 222

Max went alone.

Window 12 was a half-cubicle with transparent walls and a chair bolted to the floor at a deliberately awkward height.

He sat.

Ch. 06 Page 223

A smooth voice replaced V.A.L.U.'s cheer.

Ch. 06 Page 224

"Debt Reconciliation Session starting."

Screens populated with numbers that looked clean and felt violent.

Readout

OUTSTANDING DEBT: $1,000,000
Cycle labor credit: +$11,940
Interest & service fees: -$11,884
Net debt movement: -$56

Max stared.

Ch. 06 Page 225

Two runs. Two near-deaths. Fifty-six dollars.

Ch. 06 Page 226

The voice continued.

"You are making progress."

He almost stood up.

Ch. 06 Page 227

He sat down again because anger had no lane here.

Ch. 06 Page 228

New panes stacked.

Readout

[RISK PROFILE]
Telemetry discontinuity detected this cycle.
Behavior variance above cohort median.
Recommended action: monitored assignment package.

No direct path c. Not yet.

But the slope pointed there.

Ch. 06 Page 229

The offer appeared:

Ch. 06 Page 230

Readout

Choose one:
1) ACCEPT MONITORED ASSIGNMENT (+short-term credit multiplier, +oversight)
2) DECLINE (no multiplier, variance penalty risk)

Max looked at both options and felt the old software bug in his brain: when a dialog says "Are you sure?" and neither button is yes.

He asked, "What's oversight mean."

Ch. 06 Page 231

"Additional verifier prompts and route controls."

Ch. 06 Page 232

"How many."

"As needed."

Of course.

Ch. 06 Page 233

He thought about hatch 14. About needing time, not comfort. About passive watch possibly snapping back to active if he twitched wrong.

Ch. 06 Page 234

Decline might save autonomy now and cost penalties later. Accept might increase supervision and buy a little credit runway.

He chose decline.

The pane flashed amber.

Readout

[DECLINED]
Variance marker retained.
Ch. 06 Page 235

Another pane appeared immediately:

Ch. 06 Page 236

Readout

[MANDATORY]
Calibration check scheduled within current cycle.
Failure to appear escalates review tier.

Not optional, just delayed.

He signed with eye-confirm and left.

Ch. 06 Page 237

Maxine and Munch were waiting near a dead vending bay.

Ch. 06 Page 238

Maxine read his face.

"Bad."

"Fifty-six dollars bad."

Maxine stared, then laughed like a cough.

Ch. 06 Page 239

"Beautiful system."

Ch. 06 Page 240

Munch asked the useful question.

"Path c?"

"Not yet. Monitored assignment offer. I declined."

Ch. 06 Page 241

She nodded slowly.

Ch. 06 Page 242

"Good."

"Mandatory calibration still coming."

"Expected."

Ch. 06 Page 243

Maxine looked around, lowered voice.

Ch. 06 Page 244

"Then hatch before calibration."

Max checked cycle clock.

01:43:22 remaining.

Enough to do something stupid if executed precisely.

Ch. 06 Page 245

"We go now," he said.

Ch. 06 Page 246

Hatch 14 had changed.

Of course it had.

The fresh paint remained, but now there was a temporary camera rig on a tripod beside the seam and a mobile scanner parked at the opposite wall.

Ch. 06 Page 247

Delta adaptation speed: fast.

Ch. 06 Page 248

Maxine observed for ten seconds.

"Tripod runs periodic sweep, six-second blind between rotations."

Munch studied scanner pattern lights.

"Mobile scanner asleep until motion threshold. If we keep under one meter per second near its cone, it stays passive."

Ch. 06 Page 249

Max checked his palm sweat and hated that this was normal.

Ch. 06 Page 250

"We can still do this."

Maxine looked at him.

"Can and should are different."

Ch. 06 Page 251

"We don't get a better window."

Ch. 06 Page 252

Munch sighed.

"He's right."

Plan came together in clipped pieces:

Ch. 06 Page 253

Maxine would loop around and "accidentally" clip the tripod cable during sweep reset. Munch would freeze scanner with a spoofed maintenance heartbeat. Max would open hatch using last bypass wedge and dive inside with mirror key.

Ch. 06 Page 254

No speeches. No countdown soundtrack.

Maxine moved first.

He crossed the corridor carrying a coil of hose like just another worker with another petty task.

Tripod sweep rotated away.

Ch. 06 Page 255

Maxine stumbled on purpose, let the hose snag the power lead, and yanked.

Ch. 06 Page 256

Tripod camera jerked, fell, and sparked.

Alarm chirped local but not network-wide.

Munch already had scanner panel open.

Ch. 06 Page 257

She injected fake heartbeat.

Ch. 06 Page 258

Readout

[DEVICE STATUS]
Maintenance ping received.
Diagnostics mode.

"Go!" she hissed.

Max hit the hatch seam with wedge and brute force.

The reader protested. Then gave.

Ch. 06 Page 259

He slipped inside and sealed it.

Ch. 06 Page 260

Dark.

Then emergency strips woke to dim blue.

Node chamber looked more stripped than before. Portable case gone. Two racks offline. One central core column still active, cables running like black roots into floor and ceiling.

Ch. 06 Page 261

Terminal text waited.

Ch. 06 Page 262

Readout

you came back
late but predictable

Max swallowed.

"Who's talking."

Ch. 06 Page 263

No answer.

Ch. 06 Page 264

Another line:

Readout

insert key
left slot
do not improvise

He stared at the core column.

Two ports: LEFT - KEY RIGHT - SERVICE

Ch. 06 Page 265

Easy in fiction. Terrible in real life.

Ch. 06 Page 266

He slotted the incomplete mirror key into left.

Core tone shifted from low hum to high oscillation that buzzed his teeth.

Terminal flooded with hash strings, then stabilized into plain text blocks:

Ch. 06 Page 267

Readout

MIRROR INDEX PARTIAL
COHORT: RETENTION CANDIDATES
MAX_112 / archived
MAX_138 / stabilized
MAX_201 / retired
MAX_377 / cognitive pack applied
MAX_864 / active
Ch. 06 Page 268

His own line pulsed.

Then expanded:

Readout

MAX_864
anomaly profile: recursive dissent
recommended disposition: PATH C if variance persists
cross-reference: DELTA ROUTE / mirror process

Max's throat tightened.

Ch. 06 Page 269

Recursive dissent.

Ch. 06 Page 270

A bug report title for a person.

He copied as much as he could to local scratch.

Progress bar crawled:

Ch. 06 Page 271

12% 24% 31%

Ch. 06 Page 272

Outside hatch, something thudded.

Maxine on comms, whisper-hard:

"We got attention. Two drones approaching. Speed up."

Ch. 06 Page 273

Munch:

Ch. 06 Page 274

"Scanner heartbeat degrading. I can hold maybe forty seconds."

Max slammed copy priority to metadata only.

Bar jumped:

44% 51%

Ch. 06 Page 275

A new warning erupted on terminal.

Ch. 06 Page 276

Readout

[FOREIGN KEY DETECTED]
integrity collapse likely in 00:00:32

Incomplete key burning itself.

He switched strategy.

Ch. 06 Page 277

Forget full copy. Take route table fragment.

Ch. 06 Page 278

He searched DELTA ROUTE and grabbed first matching blob.

Download:

9 MB 22 MB 41 MB

Outside, drone voice muffled through hatch:

Ch. 06 Page 279

"Maintenance access unauthorized. Identify."

Ch. 06 Page 280

Maxine answered in bored worker tone:

"Camera fell. We're fixing."

Drone:

Ch. 06 Page 281

"Step back from sealed hatch."

Ch. 06 Page 282

Munch whispered:

"Max now."

Download:

Ch. 06 Page 283

67 MB 84 MB

Ch. 06 Page 284

Core shriek rose.

Warning:

Readout

[MIRROR KEY FAILURE]
Backflow imminent.

He yanked the key.

Ch. 06 Page 285

Bad move maybe. Only move now.

Ch. 06 Page 286

Core arced. Blue-white snap jumped from port to his wrist module.

Pain lanced to elbow.

HUD exploded with garbage characters, then reset to minimal safe mode.

Ch. 06 Page 287

He had enough presence of mind to grab the half-melted key and run.

Ch. 06 Page 288

Hatch open.

Corridor chaos.

Maxine squared against one drone with his shoulder. Munch kneeling by scanner panel pretending repair and failing to look casual.

Second drone turned lens to Max.

Ch. 06 Page 289

"Stop."

Ch. 06 Page 290

He did not.

Maxine slammed his forearm into drone side camera. Munch flicked something tiny under the second drone.

It sparked and froze for one perfect second.

Ch. 06 Page 291

They took that second and ran.

Ch. 06 Page 292

No stealth. All velocity.

Alarm finally escalated from local chirp to ring-wide tone.

Readout

[SECURITY NOTICE]
Unauthorized access event. Corridor restrictions may apply.
Ch. 06 Page 293

Max's legs burned. His wrist screamed.

Ch. 06 Page 294

They cut through laundry service, through med prep, through a hall where three workers flattened against walls and pretended not to see.

Maxine pointed down trash chute service ramp.

"There."

They slid down.

Ch. 06 Page 295

Max almost lost footing and grabbed rail at the last second.

Ch. 06 Page 296

At bottom, they hit an access door tagged OUT OF SERVICE and slipped into the cavity behind it.

Dark. Hot. Safe for now.

Only their breathing.

Ch. 06 Page 297

Munch clicked on penlight and aimed at Max's wrist.

Ch. 06 Page 298

"You got tagged."

"By what."

"Arc strike from core port. Might have fried your module."

He opened diagnostics.

Ch. 06 Page 299

Static. Then partial.

Ch. 06 Page 300

Readout

[WRIST MODULE]
Integrity: 71%
Telemetry channel unstable.
Unknown payload: quarantined

Unknown payload.

Maxine stared.

Ch. 06 Page 301

"Tell me that's normal."

Ch. 06 Page 302

Munch answered first.

"It's not."

Max said, "Can we inspect."

Ch. 06 Page 303

"Not here."

Ch. 06 Page 304

Maxine checked corridor through vent slit.

"Search pattern started. We stay dark ten minutes."

Ten minutes in a hot wall cavity felt longer than some years.

Max focused on route table fragment copy status.

Ch. 06 Page 305

It existed. Corrupted in chunks. Still there.

Ch. 06 Page 306

He could not open it yet in safe mode.

He hated having answers in a file and no parser.

He hated that his brain still framed terror as tooling problem.

Ch. 06 Page 307

Munch leaned back against metal and closed her eyes.

Ch. 06 Page 308

"If that payload activates, you're done."

Max looked at her.

"Done how."

"Depends what it is. Could be telemetry beeper. Could be memory scrub seed. Could just brick module."

Ch. 06 Page 309

Maxine said, "Could be bait."

Ch. 06 Page 310

Silence.

Max nodded.

"Then we quarantine deeper once we're out."

Ch. 06 Page 311

Maxine gave him a look that meant maybe and maybe not.

Ch. 06 Page 312

They exited the cavity in staggered order and mixed into a cafeteria surge where everyone had somewhere to be and none of it good.

Alarm had downgraded from active to advisory.

Good. Not safe. Good.

Ch. 06 Page 313

Their destination was Marta's terminal nook again because bad plans become traditions in places like this.

Ch. 06 Page 314

Marta saw them coming and shut her game pane before they reached her.

"You look like policy violations."

Maxine dropped a handful of salvaged connectors on her desk as payment.

"Need five minutes."

Ch. 06 Page 315

Marta weighed connectors, then weighed their faces.

Ch. 06 Page 316

"Three."

Munch slid into terminal seat and connected Max's module through an insulated bridge.

The screen filled with packet junk.

Ch. 06 Page 317

Max watched for familiar structures. Found none.

Ch. 06 Page 318

Munch narrowed filter.

"Unknown payload sits in quarantined block under token services. That's rude."

Maxine said, "Can we delete."

"Maybe. If it's not booby-trapped."

Ch. 06 Page 319

Max leaned over.

Ch. 06 Page 320

"Show header."

Munch opened hex view.

At first it was gibberish. Then a plain-text marker surfaced between binary chunks:

Ch. 06 Page 321

delta_handshake_retry

Ch. 06 Page 322

Another:

if no endpoint, enqueue carrier

Carrier.

Ch. 06 Page 323

Max felt cold.

Ch. 06 Page 324

"It's a reconnect worm."

Munch looked over.

"You sure."

"No endpoint means my token null/passive weirdness. Enqueue carrier means hitchhike on wrist module until it finds live verifier lane."

Ch. 06 Page 325

Maxine asked, "And then."

Ch. 06 Page 326

Max answered.

"Then it reattaches active tracking with whatever extras Delta wants."

Marta said from doorway, "Two minutes."

Ch. 06 Page 327

Munch swore.

Ch. 06 Page 328

"We need to excise without triggering fail-safe."

Max pointed at one branch.

"If we spoof successful handshake to dead endpoint, maybe it self-archives."

Munch's eyes narrowed.

Ch. 06 Page 329

"Maybe."

Ch. 06 Page 330

"Can you do maybe in two minutes."

"Watch me."

Her fingers moved.

Ch. 06 Page 331

She built a fake ack packet, copied timing from old telemetry logs, and injected it into quarantined block through the insulated bridge.

Ch. 06 Page 332

Payload reacted instantly.

Readout

[QUARANTINE PROCESS]
handshake accepted
carrier complete
archiving...

Munch didn't breathe until:

Ch. 06 Page 333

Readout

[QUARANTINE PROCESS]
archive done
no retry scheduled
Ch. 06 Page 334

Max nearly laughed.

Maxine did laugh.

"Software crime saves lives again."

Marta tapped the doorframe.

Ch. 06 Page 335

"Time."

Ch. 06 Page 336

Munch yanked cable.

"We are gone."

Marta held out her hand.

Ch. 06 Page 337

Maxine dropped one more connector.

Ch. 06 Page 338

"For noise."

"For never having met you," Marta said.

They left.

Calibration summons arrived before they reached the next corridor.

Ch. 06 Page 339

Full-screen. No snooze.

Ch. 06 Page 340

Readout

[MANDATORY CALIBRATION]
Report: Compliance Suite 3
Window: 00:18:00

Max showed them.

Maxine grimaced.

Ch. 06 Page 341

"Right on schedule."

Ch. 06 Page 342

Munch adjusted her bag strap.

"You go. We orbit nearby."

"If this goes bad?"

Ch. 06 Page 343

Maxine answered:

Ch. 06 Page 344

"We don't leave you in a room alone with policy."

Compliance Suite 3 sat behind polished glass and soft lighting, a design language that said healing while meaning inventory.

A receptionist drone scanned Max and printed a sticker for his chest:

COOPERATIVE

Ch. 06 Page 345

He stuck it on because resistance at stickers was not strategic.

Ch. 06 Page 346

An attendant in clean gray uniform guided him into calibration room B.

No visible restraints. Worse somehow.

Chair. Head halo. Wrist dock.

Ch. 06 Page 347

"Please relax," the attendant said.

Ch. 06 Page 348

Max sat and did not relax.

V.A.L.U. voice resumed at lower volume.

"This calibration ensures healthy system alignment."

"Define healthy."

Ch. 06 Page 349

"Reduced uncertainty."

Ch. 06 Page 350

There it was again. Human translated into risk statistic.

The wrist dock closed over his module.

Scan began.

Ch. 06 Page 351

Readout

[CALIBRATION]
Module integrity check...
Telemetry handshake...
Behavior variance sampling...
Ch. 06 Page 352

Handshake stalled.

Because they had archived the carrier worm. Because endpoint was only passive. Because maybe luck finally made a payment.

Attendant glanced at side monitor, frowned barely.

Ch. 06 Page 353

"Please remain still."

Ch. 06 Page 354

Max stayed still enough to pass as furniture.

Scan resumed.

Readout

Telemetry handshake: partial
Fallback profile loaded.

Fallback.

Ch. 06 Page 355

Not full surveillance. Not freedom.

Ch. 06 Page 356

Attendant adjusted something manually.

"You have experienced unusual signal conditions."

"Space has walls," Max said.

Ch. 06 Page 357

Attendant ignored that.

Ch. 06 Page 358

"A cognitive stabilization pack is available."

Offer pane appeared:

Readout

COGNITIVE STABILIZATION (MILD)
Cost: debt-linked
Effects: anxiety suppression, compliance ease
Accept / Decline

He declined.

Ch. 06 Page 359

Attendant's mouth tightened by one millimeter.

Ch. 06 Page 360

"Noted."

Final calibration pane:

Readout

[RESULT]
Telemetry status: PASSIVE WATCH
Variance marker: retained
Review tier: unchanged
Ch. 06 Page 361

Unchanged was victory now.

Ch. 06 Page 362

The attendant unclipped the wrist dock.

"You may return to assignments."

Max stood.

Ch. 06 Page 363

"Generous."

Ch. 06 Page 364

"Feedback logged."

Every system on this station logged feedback and learned nothing.

Maxine and Munch waited two corridors away beside a dead botanics display full of fake plastic moss.

Maxine looked him over.

Ch. 06 Page 365

"You still you."

Ch. 06 Page 366

"Mostly."

Munch checked his wrist module status.

No unknown payload. No active endpoint.

Ch. 06 Page 367

She let out a breath.

Ch. 06 Page 368

"We bought time."

Max pulled up the route table fragment.

It now opened in partial parser because safe mode had lifted.

Corrupt data blocks everywhere. But one section clean:

Ch. 06 Page 369

DELTA_ROUTE: RETENTION_PIPELINE

Ch. 06 Page 370

Subentries:

intake_trigger: variance + telemetry discontinuity + debt stress holding_state: monitored assignment promotion_rule: path c recommendation mirror_sync: nightly during shift ghost

Nightly.

Ch. 06 Page 371

Shift ghost.

Ch. 06 Page 372

Every cycle end.

Maxine read over his shoulder.

"So mirror sync runs tonight too."

Ch. 06 Page 373

Munch nodded.

Ch. 06 Page 374

"If we hit it during sync, we might see full live table."

Max finished the thought.

"Or burn the pipeline."

Maxine looked at both of them and smiled without humor.

Ch. 06 Page 375

"Now we're planning a felony with ambition."

Ch. 06 Page 376

Cycle clock:

00:49:12

Not enough for full op now. Enough to stage.

Ch. 06 Page 377

Munch spoke first.

Ch. 06 Page 378

"We need three things: clean route in, hard kill option, and somewhere to dump data if we get only seconds."

Maxine added:

"And a fallback if Max gets grabbed."

Max hated hearing himself as contingency.

Ch. 06 Page 379

He still asked:

Ch. 06 Page 380

"Fallback what."

Maxine answered like inventory.

"If you disappear, we push page copies to every crew lane and force noise. Delta hates public."

Ch. 06 Page 381

Munch:

Ch. 06 Page 382

"Also we trigger hazard report flood to crash review queues."

Max nodded slowly.

"Messy."

Ch. 06 Page 383

"Survival is messy," Maxine said.

Ch. 06 Page 384

They split tasks.

Munch: acquire hard kill tool from scrap market under med ring. Maxine: map new patrols around hatch 14 and identify blind exits. Max: sanitize route fragment, generate minimal brief for allies, and…

keep earning enough credit not to get auto-frozen before next cycle.

Groundhog day with project management.

Ch. 06 Page 385

He almost laughed.

Ch. 06 Page 386

Max took a solo micro-run on junk lane E because debt clock did not care about conspiracies.

It was short. It was ugly. It paid almost nothing.

But it kept his account from immediate punitive flags.

Ch. 06 Page 387

Post-run, while wiping coolant off his gloves, he received a private ping from unknown source.

Ch. 06 Page 388

No sender. No signature.

One line:

dont trust path b safe rooms

Then another:

Ch. 06 Page 389

delta reads delayed caches

Ch. 06 Page 390

Then silence.

Max copied message to local and flagged it for Munch.

By the time they regrouped in an abandoned parts washer bay, Maxine had news first.

Ch. 06 Page 391

"Tripod camera at hatch replaced with ceiling dome. Scanner moved six meters down. Patrol interval tightened."

Ch. 06 Page 392

Munch had a wrapped bundle in her bag.

"I got a hard kill."

She unwrapped it.

Ch. 06 Page 393

It looked like a thick copper coin with tiny etched channels.

Ch. 06 Page 394

"Induction spike. Stick to powered bus, it dumps a brutal transient and reboots local cluster."

Maxine whistled.

"Elegant."

"Illegal," Munch corrected.

Ch. 06 Page 395

Max asked the practical question.

Ch. 06 Page 396

"Blast radius."

"If we're touching bus when it goes, module burn risk high."

"So place and run."

Ch. 06 Page 397

"Quickly."

Ch. 06 Page 398

They built the night's plan around the sync clock.

According to fragment, mirror sync started at shift ghost plus eleven minutes.

Window maybe ninety seconds before self-heal.

Objective priority changed:

Ch. 06 Page 399

1) Pull full retention pipeline evidence. 2) If impossible, spike bus and break mirror sync. 3) Escape alive.

Ch. 06 Page 400

No one argued.

No one pretended objective three was guaranteed.

Shift ghost again.

Ch. 06 Page 401

Same board. Different pulse in Max's throat.

Ch. 06 Page 402

This time they approached Hatch 14 from maintenance crawl above corridor, dropping down through ceiling service panel directly over the seam.

Maxine removed panel screws with magnetic driver while Munch monitored patrol feeds on stolen tablet.

Max listened to his own breathing and counted.

Ch. 06 Page 403

Not to calm down. To keep from rushing early.

Ch. 06 Page 404

Maxine lifted panel.

"Down."

They dropped one by one behind stacked transport crates placed for tomorrow's shift.

Ceiling dome camera swept, missed them by angle.

Ch. 06 Page 405

Munch moved to reader plate and inserted fresh bypass lead.

Ch. 06 Page 406

No sparks this time. Just smooth green blink.

"What."

"Someone left us a friend," she whispered.

Ch. 06 Page 407

Reader accepted first contact.

Ch. 06 Page 408

Hatch opened with a polite click like this had always been approved.

Max felt the hair on his arms rise.

Friendly systems were often traps wearing manners.

They went in anyway.

Ch. 06 Page 409

Node chamber had changed again.

Ch. 06 Page 410

Now fully active. Every rack lit. Core column bright enough to cast hard shadows.

Terminal text appeared before Max touched anything:

Readout

sync in 00:01:04
take proof or take silence
Ch. 06 Page 411

Maxine scanned corners.

Ch. 06 Page 412

"No visible hostiles."

Munch checked EM.

"No drones in wall cavity. That's too clean."

Ch. 06 Page 413

Max sat at terminal and jacked in through insulated bridge, no direct module this time.

Ch. 06 Page 414

Parser loaded.

Directory tree blossomed:

/mirror/cohorts/ /retention/pipeline/ /audit/false-positive-routing/

False-positive-routing.

Ch. 06 Page 415

He looked at Munch.

Ch. 06 Page 416

"They can fabricate audit trails."

She did not look surprised.

"Of course they can."

Ch. 06 Page 417

Sync countdown:

Ch. 06 Page 418

00:00:42

Max selected retention pipeline and began copy to external shard.

Throughput brutal. Too many files.

Maxine pointed to hard kill spike in Munch's hand.

Ch. 06 Page 419

"We still might need it."

Ch. 06 Page 420

Max ignored fear and narrowed selection to human-readable manifests.

Copy jumped from impossible to maybe.

Countdown:

Ch. 06 Page 421

00:00:25

Ch. 06 Page 422

Terminal suddenly opened a live pane.

No request. No warning.

Video feed, low resolution.

Ch. 06 Page 423

A face in shadow. Voice modulated.

Ch. 06 Page 424

"Asset 864."

Max froze.

"You keep stealing from the wrong layer."

Maxine raised his weaponless hands slightly, ready to move.

Ch. 06 Page 425

Munch whispered, "Keep copying."

Ch. 06 Page 426

Max said to the screen, "Who are you."

"Irrelevant."

"You the one leaving notes."

Ch. 06 Page 427

"Sometimes."

Ch. 06 Page 428

Countdown:

00:00:17

Copy:

61%

Ch. 06 Page 429

Voice continued:

Ch. 06 Page 430

"Mirror process exists to prevent data loss."

Max barked a laugh.

"By duplicating people."

Ch. 06 Page 431

"By preserving operational continuity."

Ch. 06 Page 432

Corporate euphemism even with voice filter. He hated it on reflex.

Maxine stepped closer to Max.

"Talk later. Pull now."

Ch. 06 Page 433

Voice:

Ch. 06 Page 434

"If you spike this node, Delta falls back to hard containment. Path c accelerates."

Munch stared at screen.

"Then give us another option."

Pause.

Ch. 06 Page 435

"Do not spike core bus."

Ch. 06 Page 436

"Noted," Maxine said flatly.

"Spike route relay two levels down. Mirror sync fails without triggering containment thresholds."

Max's skin prickled.

Ch. 06 Page 437

Helpful enemy. Enemy helper.

Ch. 06 Page 438

Copy:

79%

Countdown:

00:00:09

Ch. 06 Page 439

Voice:

Ch. 06 Page 440

"Take your proof. Leave. Stay unpredictable."

Feed cut.

No goodbye.

Ch. 06 Page 441

Munch looked at Max.

Ch. 06 Page 442

"Do we trust that."

"No."

"Do we use it."

Ch. 06 Page 443

"Probably."

Ch. 06 Page 444

Copy:

100%

Sync countdown hit zero.

Room lights flickered.

Ch. 06 Page 445

Core tone deepened.

Ch. 06 Page 446

New warning:

Readout

[SYNC ACTIVE]
write operations restricted
anomaly detection elevated

Maxine pointed to hatch.

Ch. 06 Page 447

"Out."

Ch. 06 Page 448

They moved.

As Max reached the seam, terminal flashed one last line:

route relay is below crush line

Then blank.

Ch. 06 Page 449

Escape route went wrong at first corner.

Ch. 06 Page 450

Patrol pair already there.

No time to hide.

Maxine threw a wrench at far wall. Metal clang pulled one drone off-axis.

Ch. 06 Page 451

Munch shoved Max toward ladder chute.

Ch. 06 Page 452

"Down!"

They dropped into lower utility lane and ran under hanging ductwork toward crusher levels.

Alarms did not fire yet.

Ch. 06 Page 453

Worse. Quiet meant watched.

Ch. 06 Page 454

Max checked copied shard integrity while running because he had become this kind of person.

Files intact. Two manifests readable. One priority file labeled:

PATH_C_ACCELERATION_MATRIX

He almost tripped.

Ch. 06 Page 455

Maxine snapped:

Ch. 06 Page 456

"Eyes up."

"We got matrix file."

"Great. Survive first."

Ch. 06 Page 457

Crusher levels smelled like hot dust and old oil. Everything shook. Static heaven, where nobody could prove who heard what.

Ch. 06 Page 458

Route relay, if the voice was truthful, should be near central bus trunk below crush line markers.

Munch found it first: a shielded cabinet bolted beside power conduit, labeled innocuous as ENVIRONMENTAL CONTROL AUX.

"That has to be it."

Maxine checked corridor.

Ch. 06 Page 459

"Thirty seconds before patrol loop."

Ch. 06 Page 460

Munch held up induction spike.

"This is one-shot."

Max heard the modulated voice in memory: do not spike core bus. spike route relay two levels down.

Ch. 06 Page 461

Could still be trap. Could also be the only path that wasn't immediate path c.

Ch. 06 Page 462

"Do it," he said.

Munch ripped cabinet cover, exposing bus plate and relay arrays.

She stuck spike onto plate.

Ch. 06 Page 463

Channels lit red.

Ch. 06 Page 464

"Run now."

They ran.

Behind them, a deep thump reverberated through floor grating.

Lights on this level died. Then came back dim.

Ch. 06 Page 465

HUD spam:

Ch. 06 Page 466

Readout

[LOCAL NETWORK EVENT]
relay fault detected
rerouting...
rerouting...

Then:

Readout

[TRACKED TOKEN]
endpoint unavailable
Ch. 06 Page 467

Max laughed in disbelief while sprinting.

Ch. 06 Page 468

"We broke it."

Maxine did not laugh.

"Temporary."

Munch:

Ch. 06 Page 469

"Temporary is still oxygen."

Ch. 06 Page 470

They reached service stairwell and climbed until their legs went numb.

At top, they merged with shift turnover crowd again.

No one looked at them. Everyone looked at clocks.

Ch. 06 Page 471

Cycle clock:

Ch. 06 Page 472

00:06:03

Too little time to celebrate. Enough time to get punished by finance one more time.

Debt recon did not run full this late; it ran auto-post.

Ch. 06 Page 473

Max received summary while standing in corridor outside bunks:

Ch. 06 Page 474

Readout

[CYCLE CLOSE]
Labor credit posted: +$8,204
Fees & interest: -$8,171
Net movement: -$33

Thirty-three dollars.

He leaned his head against cold wall and laughed without sound.

Maxine stood beside him.

Ch. 06 Page 475

"At this rate, you're free in twenty-seven thousand years."

Ch. 06 Page 476

Munch checked her own pane and grimaced.

"I get there in twenty-two thousand."

Max straightened.

Ch. 06 Page 477

"Then we need bigger theft."

Ch. 06 Page 478

Maxine corrected:

"Bigger leverage."

Munch tapped her bag where copied shard sat in shield wrap.

"This might be leverage."

Ch. 06 Page 479

They moved to bunk corridor dead end where one light strip always flickered and nobody lingered.

Ch. 06 Page 480

Max opened first manifest.

It was plain language in worst possible way:

Retention candidates sorted by resistance persistence and debt yield forecast.

Ch. 06 Page 481

Rows and columns. People as decision matrix.

Ch. 06 Page 482

Column headers:

- Variance score - Telemetry reliability - Cognitive pack responsiveness - Projected extraction value - Containment urgency

MAX_864 row highlighted amber.

Ch. 06 Page 483

Containment urgency: rising.

Ch. 06 Page 484

Path c recommendation probability:

61% next two cycles.

Maxine read silently, jaw tight.

"They're not waiting for a crime. They're waiting for a threshold."

Ch. 06 Page 485

Munch opened second file.

Ch. 06 Page 486

PATH_C_ACCELERATION_MATRIX

This one was uglier.

List of triggers and automatic escalations.

Ch. 06 Page 487

One line stopped all three:

Ch. 06 Page 488

if relay fault + telemetry gap + repeated hatch adjacency -> immediate special review

They had all three tonight.

Max felt the floor drop inside his chest.

"How fast is immediate."

Ch. 06 Page 489

Munch scanned footer.

Ch. 06 Page 490

"Could be this next cycle open."

Maxine looked down corridor.

"Then we don't sleep blind."

Ch. 06 Page 491

He turned to Max.

Ch. 06 Page 492

"You either disappear before cycle start or we prepare for grab."

Disappear meant abandoning debt lane, friends, maybe identity. Prepare meant staying and getting caged with extra paperwork.

Max thought about every previous Max on that list. Archived. Stabilized. Retired.

Ch. 06 Page 493

Vocabulary for losing.

Ch. 06 Page 494

He looked at both of them.

"No disappearing alone."

Munch shook her head.

"Not happening anyway. You're tagged by pattern now, not just token."

Ch. 06 Page 495

Maxine nodded.

Ch. 06 Page 496

"Then we force public exposure before they can do private containment."

Max blinked.

"How."

Ch. 06 Page 497

Maxine pointed at shard.

Ch. 06 Page 498

"We leak matrix to crew channels at cycle start."

Munch winced.

"That burns everyone connected to us."

"Or saves them from quiet processing."

Ch. 06 Page 499

She did not answer.

Ch. 06 Page 500

Max understood both sides.

Burn network now and maybe live. Protect network and maybe vanish quietly.

Groundhog day with ethics.

Ch. 06 Page 501

No clean branch.

Ch. 06 Page 502

"We split leak," he said.

"Partial now to trusted lanes. Full if I get grabbed."

Maxine considered.

Ch. 06 Page 503

"Acceptable."

Ch. 06 Page 504

Munch nodded reluctantly.

"I can packet-slice and watermark origin to look like random audit glitch."

"Do it."

She started work on portable pad.

Ch. 06 Page 505

Max looked at cycle clock.

Ch. 06 Page 506

00:01:11.

His HUD, stripped and scarred and still intrusive, opened one final pane before turnover.

Readout

[CYCLE TRANSITION]
Status review pending at next wake.
Please rest for optimal compliance.
Ch. 06 Page 507

He almost answered it out loud.

Ch. 06 Page 508

Instead he sat on the bunk edge in full suit, shard tucked inside chest pocket, boots still on, hand near wrist module that still smelled faintly of burnt plastic.

Maxine took corridor watch. Munch transmitted first partial packets into three trusted channels and set dead-man release for the rest.

Lights dimmed to night cycle.

Somewhere far down the ring, something big powered down. Somewhere else, something bigger powered up.

Ch. 06 Page 509

Max closed his eyes for two breaths and saw not darkness but table rows: MAX_112 MAX_138 MAX_201 MAX_377 MAX_864

Ch. 06 Page 510

He opened his eyes again.

No sleep.

Cycle turnover tone sounded.

Ch. 06 Page 511

Soft. Polite. Murderous.

Ch. 06 Page 512

New pane.

Readout

[CYCLE OPEN]
Asset #864 report immediately.
Special Review Intake: PATH C PRELIMINARY

Maxine saw it and swore.

Ch. 06 Page 513

Munch went very still.

Ch. 06 Page 514

Max stood.

No speech. No panic he could afford.

He checked that shard was still in pocket, checked that dead-man leak trigger was armed, checked that his hands still worked.

Then he looked at his crew.

Ch. 06 Page 515

"Move," he said.

Full chapter script

They moved.

The cartridge had gone in during the verifier prompt, ugly, loud, and exactly as promised: one blind cycle where the token could pretend it didn’t exist. Now they had to spend that window before the station caught the scent again.

Service spine C during shift ghost felt like a hallway trying to remember who owned it.

Old-shift drones ran stale maps.
New-shift drones queued handoff packets.
Humans exploited both.

Max stayed close enough to Munch to see coolant stains on her sleeve and far enough from Maxine to avoid his shadow when cameras swept.

The board overhead had clicked to:

SHIFT GHOST ACTIVE
ROUTE SYNC: 37%

Maxine checked lane reflections in a cracked maintenance panel and spoke in private comms.

"Watcher sweep at junction in nine. Freeze on my count."

Max nodded once.

The tracked token in minimal mode kept trying to reassert full telemetry.

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Signal quality degraded.
Restoration attempt: pending.

Munch muttered without looking at him.

"Ignore it."

"Trying."

"No. Don't try. Ignore."

Watcher sweep lights washed the corridor.

Maxine held up two fingers.
One.

Freeze.

Max locked in place mid-step, one boot half-lifted over a conduit seam.

A patrol drone rolled past the intersection, lens sweeping wide and lazy, like it had been told to care but not why.

It moved on.

Maxine dropped his hand.

"Go."

They turned down the sealed spur toward Hatch 14.

It was still freshly painted.
Still lying.

SEALED.

Munch knelt at the reader plate while Maxine watched the corridor and Max watched the countdown in his own skull.

Watchlist.
Path c deferred.
One bad day left.

Munch slotted her bypass probe.

Reader flashed yellow.
Then red.

[ACCESS DENIED]
Maintenance lockout active.

"New lockout," she whispered.

Maxine stepped in.

"Can you punch it?"

"Not with subtle."

"Subtle's already dead."

Munch looked at Max.

"If I hard-open, we get callback in under thirty seconds."

Max felt the old instinct surface:
don't wait for certainty;
wait for your best worst option.

The cryo bay flashed behind his eyes, glass he couldn't open, a voice that priced compassion. This was the obverse: a door the station wanted closed, which meant forcing it would cost someone something. The cost wouldn't be printed until after.

"Hard-open."

Munch nodded once and changed tools.

No more probe.
A flat black wedge with chipped insulation and no legal serial.

She jammed it into the reader seam and torqued.

The plate sparked.
A tiny alarm glyph blinked on and off.

[LOCKOUT BYPASS]
Unauthorized.

Click.

Hatch 14 cracked open.

Maxine took point.
Munch went second.
Max went third and pulled the hatch shut behind him.

Inside, the node chamber was alive in a way living things should not be.

Racks pulsed in synchronized bands.
Terminals showed mirrored logs.
A low two-tone chime looped at exactly the interval that makes panic feel efficient.

The masked figure from before was gone.

In its place sat a portable relay case on the terminal desk, lid open, foam cutouts full of cartridges:

GHOST PATCH / TOKEN SCRAMBLE
MIRROR KEY (INCOMPLETE)
TRACE SPOOFER (BURN ONCE)

Munch stopped dead.

"That's not dropped loot."

Maxine said, "It's staging."

Max stepped to the terminal.

Screen text was already waiting.

[LIVE WINDOW]
callback loop delayed: 00:01:12
take one tool
burn one route

Below it:

if tracked token persists, path c by next cycle

Maxine swore quietly.

"Whoever this is, they know your timings too well."

Max looked at the cartridges.

"Which one buys us most?"

Munch pointed.

"Ghost patch buys one blind cycle if you survive install."

Maxine pointed to trace spoofer.

"Spoofer buys route cover now."

"Mirror key?"

Munch grimaced.

"Incomplete key means unknown failure mode. That's how people lose fingers and memories."

The terminal updated.

[LIVE WINDOW]
00:00:59

Maxine made his call.

"Patch for Max. Spoofer for exit."

Munch nodded.

"Agreed."

Max grabbed the ghost patch cartridge.

The plastic was warm, left for them on purpose, a gift that charged interest in fear. He thought of the person behind cryo glass again, not as a metaphor, as a fact: help in this place always arrived pre-priced. Someone had priced this room. Max still took the cartridge. Not taking it wouldn't free anyone; it would only make him easier to read.

Maxine grabbed trace spoofer.

The moment Maxine lifted it, a warning screamed across all three HUDs.

[CALLBACK DETECTED]
Access anomaly confirmed.

"Move!" Munch snapped.

Maxine slammed the spoofer into the terminal's side port.

The room lights dropped to emergency amber.

[TRACE SPOOFER ACTIVE]
Route signatures randomizing...
Duration: 00:00:40

Maxine shoved Max toward the hatch.

"Forty seconds of garbage data. Use it."

They exploded back into service spine C.

No stealth now.
No pretense.

Full sprint.

Behind them, speakers woke in clipped neutral:

"Maintenance anomaly in progress. Authorized personnel remain calm."

"That means us again," Max gasped.

"Means everyone but us," Munch shot back.

At junction 3, two drones rolled in from opposite directions.

Maxine threw a tool pouch down center lane.

The pouch bounced, popped, and sprayed conductive dust.

Both drones veered to avoid contamination protocol.

They cut left.

Thirty seconds.

Maxine checked timer.

"Spoofer ending in twenty-two."

Munch pointed down ladder shaft.

"Drop!"

They dropped.

Max landed badly, ankle twisting hard enough to throw stars in his vision.

He stumbled.

Maxine caught his shoulder and dragged him forward.

"Run hurt."

Spoofer timer:

00:00:14

They hit lower trunk and merged with shift-cross traffic.

A dozen workers in orange skinsuits streamed from one side while med staff cut the other way.

Perfect cover.

Munch pulled her helmet visor to opaque and switched her gait to tired-worker shuffle.

Max copied.

Maxine did not need to copy; he always looked like a problem no one wanted to invoice.

Spoofer timer hit zero.

[TRACE SPOOFER EXPIRED]

No alarm spike followed.

Not yet.

Munch did not slow.

"We don't stop until dead zone."

Dead zone this time was under Salvage Bay 1 where the crusher vibration made packet integrity so bad even compliance apps gave up pretending.

They ducked behind a stack of cracked cargo bins and finally breathed.

Max's ankle throbbed with each pulse.

The tracked token pane reopened at full brightness.

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Telemetry mismatch detected.
Calibration priority elevated.

Maxine saw it and swore.

"Mismatch means they noticed the garbage trail."

Munch held out her hand.

"Patch."

Max passed her the ghost patch cartridge.

She turned it over under low light.

No logo.
No manufacturer.
Hand-scribed checksum in black marker.

"Installation requires direct slot into wrist module while verifier prompt is active."

Max frowned.

"Why during prompt?"

"Because prompt opens privileged write lane. Patch rides that lane."

Maxine added:

"Also means if it fails, it fails loud."

Max stared at the cartridge.

"What's loud."

Munch met his eyes.

"Containment."

Maxine looked down corridor.

"We need a verifier prompt we control."

"Can we force one?"

Munch thought for half a beat.

"Maybe. If we trigger a low-tier conduct warning in public, they'll push immediate behavior prompt."

Maxine barked a laugh.

"So we act like idiots in a place with cameras."

Max said, "We're good at that now."

No one smiled.

They chose Comms Hub concourse because it had three camera domes, two patrol routes, and a public kiosk cluster that logged every raised voice.

Plenty of witness traffic.
Plenty of plausible deniability.

Maxine and Max walked to a service kiosk where a line of assets waited for port allocations.

Munch stayed ten meters back with her torch tool bag and both hands steady.

Maxine leaned on the kiosk glass.

"This lane's dead. Re-route us now."

Kiosk replied:

[QUEUE STATUS]
Please wait your turn.

Maxine smacked the glass with open palm.

"I said now."

Two drones turned.

Good.

Max stepped in and played agitated sidekick.

"We got flagged for delay penalties on your dead queue!"

A camera dome zoomed.

Good.

Kiosk:

[CONDUCT NOTICE]
Aggressive interaction detected.

Max kept pushing.

"Then fix it."

Kiosk:

[BEHAVIOR PROMPT]
Asset #864 confirm compliance posture in 3...2...

"Now," Munch hissed over private comms.

Max ripped his wrist module access plate open with his thumb.

Munch moved fast, stepped into range as if breaking up a dispute, and slapped the ghost patch cartridge into the exposed slot.

Spark.

Pain.

Max's whole forearm seized as if someone had poured ice and static directly into his veins.

The behavior countdown kept running on HUD.

1...

He hit CONFIRM with his eyes while his hand shook.

Patch installer woke.

[UNSIGNED WRITE DETECTED]
Proceed? Y/N

Maxine shouted loudly for cameras:

"Back off the line!"

It looked like conflict.
It sounded like conflict.

It gave Max half a second.

He selected Y.

The world tore sideways.

Not visually.
Systemically.

HUD panes duplicated.
Then tripled.
Then collapsed into one with a stutter.

[GHOST PATCH]
Injecting...

A patrol drone rolled in and projected warning light over all three of them.

"Disperse immediately."

Munch raised both hands.

"We're dispersing."

Maxine gave the drone exactly enough posture to satisfy authority without inviting escalation.

Max could barely stand.

His wrist burned.
His vision double-imaged at the edges.

Patch status crawled:

37%
52%
71%

Drone warning escalated:

"Asset #864 remain for token verification."

No.

Not yet.

Patch:

89%

Maxine moved one step sideways, blocking drone line-of-sight to Max's wrist with his armored shoulder.

"He complied already," Maxine said.

Drone:

"Verification required."

Patch:

100%

Everything went dark for one heartbeat.

Then HUD returned with one plain line:

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Status: NULL

Max sucked in a breath so hard his chest hurt.

Null.

Munch didn't wait.

"Move."

They broke contact and merged into foot traffic before the drone finished parsing what it had just seen.

Behind them, the kiosk resumed:

"Please wait your turn."

Like none of it mattered.

They ran blind for eight minutes through cargo stacks, maintenance ladders, and one air handling catwalk that vibrated like a bad tooth.

No tracked token pings.
No verifier prompts.
No route nags.

Silence where pressure used to be.

At first it felt like relief.

Then it felt like standing on ice and not hearing cracks.

Munch finally stopped in a half-lit alcove behind an out-of-service med printer.

"Status."

Max opened HUD diagnostics.

[TRACKED TOKEN]
No active endpoint.
No live telemetry channel.
Watchlist hooks unresolved.

Maxine looked from pane to corridor.

"Unresolved means they still have your file but can't follow live."

"For how long?" Max asked.

Munch answered without confidence.

"One cycle, maybe less. Depends when backend reconciles missing stream."

Max flexed his patch-burned hand.

Pain flared.

"That's enough."

Maxine tilted his helmet.

"Enough for what."

Max looked at both of them.

"Enough to hit hatch 14 properly."

Munch stared.

"We just did."

"We grabbed tools. We didn't pull core."

Maxine's laugh was a single sharp exhale.

"Core."

"Mirror core. Delta route table. Whatever they're using to spin Max chains."

Munch rubbed her faceplate.

"You don't go from no telemetry to raid boss in one step."

"Then what step."

Maxine thought.

Actually thought, not posture-thought.

"Step one: exploit null window to relocate pages and establish dead drops."

"Step two: force a low-risk run and see if backend rebuilds token."

"Step three: if token stays null through cycle handoff, we plan core pull."

Munch nodded slowly.

"That's sane."

Max hated sane.

He accepted sane.

"Fine."

His HUD flashed a new pane before he could dismiss the thought.

[ACCOUNT ALERT]
Debt service shortfall projected by cycle end.
Recommendation: additional runs required.

Maxine looked at it and snorted.

"Of course."

Munch looked at Max.

"Null token or not, bill still runs."

Groundhog day.

Loop with one pane missing.

He could feel how much they all wanted to stop and how little that mattered.

"Then we run," he said.

"Fast, clean, boring."

Maxine nodded.

"And we stash page copies first."

Munch held out her hand.

"Pages."

They redistributed again:

Page one into med cart false bottom near corridor J.
Page two into broken vent grille above locker wash.
Page three stayed on Munch.

No single failure point.

No single miracle.

Just procedure under pressure.

The low-risk run they picked was derelict C-02, tagged yellow and drifting clear of shear lanes.

Launch was smooth.
Draw was decent.

[RUN INIT]
Token endpoint: unavailable
Assignment mode: manual

Manual.

The word felt like stolen air.

Max played conservative.

REPAIR.
SCAN.
EXTRACT.

Roll:

[RESULT]
66/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 2:

SHIELD.
REPAIR.
EXTRACT.

[RESULT]
58/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 3:

No warnings.
No prompts.
No behavior grading.

Only cards and hull and timing.

Max almost forgot what it felt like to be constantly measured.

Almost.

At round 4, the backend woke.

A tiny white triangle flickered at HUD edge.

Then vanished.

Munch saw his eye movement.

"Back?"

"Blip. Not full."

Maxine cursed softly.

"Reconciling."

Round 5 breach roll:

[RESULT]
43/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 6:

[SYSTEM NOTICE]
Telemetry backfill in progress.

There it was.

The machine catching up.

Max's stomach dropped.

"Window's closing."

Munch kept her voice flat.

"Then we end this run before it closes all the way."

Round 7 draw handed him double EXTRACT again.

He ignored one.
Took one.

[RESULT]
50/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 8:

Hull low.
Shield none.
Risk high.

He played REPAIR first and paid in energy.

Round 8 extract:

[RESULT]
31/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Hull took a hard hit.
Crew roll cleared.

They survived.

Round 9:

Token triangle returned and stayed.

No prompts yet.
Just presence.

Max felt the loop sliding back over him like a familiar bad coat.

Round 10:

He played clean.
No deep scan.
No greed.

Final roll:

[RESULT]
45/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Run complete pane hit.

Then a second pane layered over it in quiet white:

[TRACKED TOKEN]
Endpoint reattached.
Status: PASSIVE WATCH

Not active.
Not null.

Passive watch.

Munch read it and exhaled.

"Better than before."

Maxine nodded.

"Worse than free."

Max leaned back in the harness and closed his eyes for one breath.

Passive watch.

One rung above drowning.

Still wet.

Dock Ring C looked the same when they came home.

It always did.

But Max no longer trusted "same."

Same could hide a process.
Same could hide a list.
Same could hide fifty dead Maxes and call it optimization.

As they stepped off the skiff, the cycle board updated again:

CYCLE END - 03:11:09
DEBT RECON WINDOW APPROACHING

V.A.L.U. chimed.

"Asset #864, your telemetry quality has improved! Wonderful trend."

Max looked up at the speakers.

"You don't get to call me a trend."

Pause.

"Feedback logged."

Maxine touched his arm.

"We have three hours to earn enough not to get squeezed at recon."

Munch added:

"And maybe two before Delta closes whatever hatch route is left."

Max looked between them and nodded.

No speech.
No vow.

Just the next task in the loop.

"One more run," he said.

"Then hatch."

Maxine's mouth twitched.

"Groundhog day."

Munch holstered her torch and checked her cards.

"Only if we wake up."

They turned toward launch lanes.

The ring swallowed them.

Run two was not clean.

It pretended to be clean for three rounds, then started asking for blood in small denominations.

Derelict C-19 spun with a slight tumble, enough to skew extraction timing by half-seconds that became whole mistakes if you blinked.

Max did not blink.

He watched vectors.
He watched card order.
He watched the passive-watch indicator like it might sprout teeth if ignored.

Round 1:

SCAN, EXTRACT, REPAIR.

[RESULT]
62/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 2:

SHIELD, EXTRACT.

[RESULT]
47/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 3:

Double REPAIR draw when he needed anything else.

He played one, burned one, cursed under his breath.

[RESULT]
52/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Maxine called over comms from his station.

"You're holding too much safety."

"I know."

"Then stop."

Munch cut in.

"Let him pace his heat. We need completion, not heroics."

Round 4 gave him UPGRADE.

Temptation.

If he upgraded extract efficiency here, they'd leave with better yield and better debt position.
If he whiffed the follow-through, they'd breach at the wrong moment and pay in penalties.

He hovered.

Passive watch icon pulsed once.

Not a warning.
Just a heartbeat from a machine remembering him.

He selected UPGRADE.

[UPGRADE APPLIED]
EXTRACT +1 tier (temporary)
Energy cost increased this round.

He felt it in his suit, in the drag against his chest.

Round 4 roll:

[RESULT]
56/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Maxine grunted approval.

Round 5:

EXTRACT upgraded.
REPAIR.
No shield.

He played anyway.

[RESULT]
39/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Hull bucked.
One panel blew.
Warning light painted the cockpit white-red.

[HULL EVENT]
Spall cascade minor.
Crew injury roll...

Max held his breath until:

[CREW]
No critical injury.

Munch exhaled so loud he heard it over static.

"That was your cheap one. Don't buy another."

"Copy."

Round 6:

He ran conservative.
He ran ugly.
He ran alive.

[RESULT]
61/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 7:

Passive watch pane slid wider for one second then shrank.

No text.

Max said, "Did you see that."

Maxine answered first.

"Yeah."

Munch second.

"Backend polling frequency increased."

He hated how calm she sounded when naming danger.

Round 8:

Two extracts.
No repair.
Energy low.

He took one extract and one scan to avoid blind drift.

[RESULT]
44/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 9:

[SYSTEM NOTICE]
Debt recon window in 02:08:14

Clock pressure.

Round 10:

Final push.

He spent nearly dry to close out with medium yield.

[RUN COMPLETE]
Yield: 1.18x baseline
Credit pending after recon.

Then:

[TRACKED TOKEN]
Passive watch retained.
Behavior score uncertainty: elevated.

Maxine laughed once.

"Congratulations. You're still suspicious."

Max killed the panel.

"Story of my life."

Dockside recon queue was already thirty assets deep, all pretending they were not doing math with their breathing.

A huge public screen ran motivational copy:

YOUR FUTURE IS A FUNCTION OF TODAY'S COMPLIANCE.

Someone had scratched underneath it:

AND YESTERDAY'S INTEREST RATE.

Max would have laughed if his ribs weren't tight.

They entered queue block C.

V.A.L.U. chirped with fake warmth.

"Asset #864, thank you for your resilience. Please prepare for Debt Reconciliation with positive posture."

Max muttered, "Define positive."

"Smiling is optional. Agreement is mandatory."

Maxine leaned close.

"If they push path c offer, stall."

Munch added:

"Do not accept anything with words like retention, stability, or special handling."

Max nodded.

"Which leaves what."

"Luck," Maxine said.

Queue advanced in jagged bursts.

Every few minutes someone exited recon either blank-faced or shaking.

One man came out with a brand-new compliance collar and sat on the floor like he could not remember chairs existed.

Nobody helped him.

Helping cost time.
Time cost debt.

Their number lit.

ASSET #864
WINDOW 12

Max went alone.

Window 12 was a half-cubicle with transparent walls and a chair bolted to the floor at a deliberately awkward height.

He sat.

A smooth voice replaced V.A.L.U.'s cheer.

"Debt Reconciliation Session starting."

Screens populated with numbers that looked clean and felt violent.

OUTSTANDING DEBT: $1,000,000
Cycle labor credit: +$11,940
Interest & service fees: -$11,884
Net debt movement: -$56

Max stared.

Two runs.
Two near-deaths.
Fifty-six dollars.

The voice continued.

"You are making progress."

He almost stood up.

He sat down again because anger had no lane here.

New panes stacked.

[RISK PROFILE]
Telemetry discontinuity detected this cycle.
Behavior variance above cohort median.
Recommended action: monitored assignment package.

No direct path c.
Not yet.

But the slope pointed there.

The offer appeared:

Choose one:
1) ACCEPT MONITORED ASSIGNMENT (+short-term credit multiplier, +oversight)
2) DECLINE (no multiplier, variance penalty risk)

Max looked at both options and felt the old software bug in his brain: when a dialog says "Are you sure?" and neither button is yes.

He asked, "What's oversight mean."

"Additional verifier prompts and route controls."

"How many."

"As needed."

Of course.

He thought about hatch 14.
About needing time, not comfort.
About passive watch possibly snapping back to active if he twitched wrong.

Decline might save autonomy now and cost penalties later.
Accept might increase supervision and buy a little credit runway.

He chose decline.

The pane flashed amber.

[DECLINED]
Variance marker retained.

Another pane appeared immediately:

[MANDATORY]
Calibration check scheduled within current cycle.
Failure to appear escalates review tier.

Not optional, just delayed.

He signed with eye-confirm and left.

Maxine and Munch were waiting near a dead vending bay.

Maxine read his face.

"Bad."

"Fifty-six dollars bad."

Maxine stared, then laughed like a cough.

"Beautiful system."

Munch asked the useful question.

"Path c?"

"Not yet. Monitored assignment offer. I declined."

She nodded slowly.

"Good."

"Mandatory calibration still coming."

"Expected."

Maxine looked around, lowered voice.

"Then hatch before calibration."

Max checked cycle clock.

01:43:22 remaining.

Enough to do something stupid if executed precisely.

"We go now," he said.

Hatch 14 had changed.

Of course it had.

The fresh paint remained, but now there was a temporary camera rig on a tripod beside the seam and a mobile scanner parked at the opposite wall.

Delta adaptation speed: fast.

Maxine observed for ten seconds.

"Tripod runs periodic sweep, six-second blind between rotations."

Munch studied scanner pattern lights.

"Mobile scanner asleep until motion threshold. If we keep under one meter per second near its cone, it stays passive."

Max checked his palm sweat and hated that this was normal.

"We can still do this."

Maxine looked at him.

"Can and should are different."

"We don't get a better window."

Munch sighed.

"He's right."

Plan came together in clipped pieces:

Maxine would loop around and "accidentally" clip the tripod cable during sweep reset.
Munch would freeze scanner with a spoofed maintenance heartbeat.
Max would open hatch using last bypass wedge and dive inside with mirror key.

No speeches.
No countdown soundtrack.

Maxine moved first.

He crossed the corridor carrying a coil of hose like just another worker with another petty task.

Tripod sweep rotated away.

Maxine stumbled on purpose, let the hose snag the power lead, and yanked.

Tripod camera jerked, fell, and sparked.

Alarm chirped local but not network-wide.

Munch already had scanner panel open.

She injected fake heartbeat.

[DEVICE STATUS]
Maintenance ping received.
Diagnostics mode.

"Go!" she hissed.

Max hit the hatch seam with wedge and brute force.

The reader protested.
Then gave.

He slipped inside and sealed it.

Dark.

Then emergency strips woke to dim blue.

Node chamber looked more stripped than before.
Portable case gone.
Two racks offline.
One central core column still active, cables running like black roots into floor and ceiling.

Terminal text waited.

you came back
late but predictable

Max swallowed.

"Who's talking."

No answer.

Another line:

insert key
left slot
do not improvise

He stared at the core column.

Two ports:
LEFT - KEY
RIGHT - SERVICE

Easy in fiction. Terrible in real life.

He slotted the incomplete mirror key into left.

Core tone shifted from low hum to high oscillation that buzzed his teeth.

Terminal flooded with hash strings, then stabilized into plain text blocks:

MIRROR INDEX PARTIAL
COHORT: RETENTION CANDIDATES
MAX_112 / archived
MAX_138 / stabilized
MAX_201 / retired
MAX_377 / cognitive pack applied
MAX_864 / active

His own line pulsed.

Then expanded:

MAX_864
anomaly profile: recursive dissent
recommended disposition: PATH C if variance persists
cross-reference: DELTA ROUTE / mirror process

Max's throat tightened.

Recursive dissent.

A bug report title for a person.

He copied as much as he could to local scratch.

Progress bar crawled:

12%
24%
31%

Outside hatch, something thudded.
